classes.name: Barbarian
classes.description: Barbarians are front-line warriors whose power rises from rage, instinct, endurance, and mythic physical presence rather than disciplined technique alone. In a party they usually serve as durable strikers, living shields, and momentum breakers who force enemies to answer them first. Their core fantasy is the hero who survives impossible wounds, crosses the battlefield through raw will, and turns emotion into a weapon. A barbarian can be a tribal guardian, gladiator, cursed wanderer, revolutionary, raider, monster hunter, or gentle giant who becomes terrifying only when loved ones are threatened. Mechanically, the class favors Strength, Constitution, weapon attacks, mobility, and high-risk aggression. Narratively, it works best when rage is not treated as mindless anger, but as ancestral memory, survival response, sacred fury, battle trance, or the voice of a harsh world speaking through the body. In a generator, this class should influence combat role, voice posture, equipment, duties, common conflicts, and how an NPC reacts when danger interrupts ordinary life.

subclasses.name: Berserker
subclasses.description: Path of the Berserker is built around unrestrained fury, frenzy, and the brutal fantasy of a warrior who burns through exhaustion and fear to keep attacking. It gives the barbarian a sharper identity than the base class alone, turning ordinary training into a specific tradition, philosophy, curse, talent, or calling. In play, this subclass emphasizes direct melee pressure with simple choices, high damage, intimidation, and risk-reward endurance. The character’s decisions should therefore feel different from another barbarian with the same core features, because this path changes how they approach danger, allies, preparation, and victory. For story use, it easily supports a raider, pit fighter, berserk champion, bodyguard, or traumatized survivor whose rage becomes momentum. DMs can attach mentors, rivals, orders, taboos, ceremonies, reputations, and personal costs to that concept. For players, it rewards committing to the fantasy instead of treating the subclass as a passive bonus: describe how the power looks, what habits it creates, and what enemies notice when it appears. In an NPC generator, this subclass should influence goals, equipment, social role, downtime work, and battlefield behavior. Allies should know what this person is trusted to do, while enemies should understand why meeting them is dangerous. This entry is associated with Player's Handbook in common 5e reference use, but this summary is original and avoids reproducing official rules text. The rage should feel like a doorway into this path’s myth, not just a damage switch.
keywords: the brutal fantasy of a warrior who burns through exhaustion

classes.name: Bard
classes.description: Bards are versatile artists, speakers, historians, spies, duelists, diplomats, and magical performers whose power comes through story, rhythm, inspiration, and social awareness. They are rarely limited to one battlefield role: a bard can heal, hinder, charm, scout, negotiate, blast, or support depending on their chosen college and spell selection. Their defining strength is adaptability, especially in campaigns that reward skill use, roleplay, improvisation, and problem solving outside of simple combat. A bard may be a court poet, revolutionary singer, traveling comedian, scholar of lost epics, battlefield drummer, spirit medium, silver-tongued envoy, or blade-dancer whose performance is also a weapon. Mechanically, they lean on Charisma, skills, Bardic Inspiration, and flexible spellcasting. Narratively, bards remind the table that culture is power: names, songs, rumors, speeches, and memories can change kingdoms as surely as swords. classes.name: Cleric

They are not simply healers; they can become armored front-line champions, radiant destroyers, battlefield commanders, scholars, exorcists, peacemakers, storm-callers, grave wardens, or trickster-priests depending on their domain. In a party, clerics often provide durability, restoration, protection, utility, and decisive magic against undead or supernatural threats. Their stories can involve temples, pilgrimages, heresies, vows, miracles, doubt, holy politics, and the burden of representing a power larger than themselves. Mechanically, they rely on Wisdom, prepared spells, domain features, and strong support options. A cleric’s subclass is especially identity-defining because the domain explains what kind of divinity or sacred principle answers when the character prays. classes.name: Monk

They are swift, mobile, precise, and often strange to enemies who expect steel and armor rather than open hands, pressure points, impossible leaps, or inner power. In a party, monks often act as skirmishers, disruptors, scouts, and mage-hunters who reach vulnerable targets and interfere with enemy plans. A monk may come from a monastery, street-fighting lineage, hidden temple, assassin school, wandering healer order, philosophical tradition, or personal discipline developed in isolation. Mechanically, monks depend on Dexterity, Wisdom, mobility, bonus actions, ki or focus resources, and careful positioning. Narratively, their subclasses define the philosophy behind their training: mercy, shadow, elemental harmony, weapon perfection, drunken misdirection, astral manifestation, or mastery over death itself. classes.name: Ranger

classes.name: Rogue

classes.name: Sorcerer
classes.description: Sorcerers are innate spellcasters whose magic comes from bloodline, exposure, destiny, cosmic accident, divine touch, planar influence, aberrant mutation, storm blessing, shadow corruption, or reality’s strange favor. Unlike wizards, they do not primarily study magic from books; their power is part of them, often emotional, physical, hereditary, or uncontrollable. In a party, sorcerers usually function as flexible arcane casters, blasters, controllers, faces, and specialists who reshape spells through metamagic. Their stories often involve identity, inheritance, fear of power, family secrets, transformation, and the question of whether a gift is also a curse. Mechanically, sorcerers lean on Charisma, limited spells known, sorcery points, and spell modification. Their subclass matters deeply because it explains why magic answers them and what kind of impossible force is trying to speak through their body. In a generator, this class should influence combat role, voice posture, equipment, duties, common conflicts, and how an NPC reacts when danger interrupts ordinary life.

classes.name: Warlock
classes.description: Warlocks are pact-bound spellcasters who gain power through bargains, patronage, forbidden study, stolen secrets, or contact with entities beyond ordinary mortal reach. Their patrons may be fiends, archfey, ancient stars, sentient weapons, genies, celestials, undead powers, sea horrors, or other supernatural forces. In a party, warlocks often act as blasters, faces, utility casters, occult investigators, and strange specialists with customizable invocations. Their magic feels different from other casters because it is negotiated, branded, gifted, or owed. A warlock’s story can revolve around debt, temptation, loopholes, devotion, exploitation, rebellion, or partnership with the patron. Mechanically, they lean on Charisma, pact magic, short-rest spell recovery, Eldritch Blast-style consistency, invocations, and pact boons. Narratively, the best warlocks are not just “evil mages”; they are people who asked something dangerous for help and now live with the answer.
